column15: Avg. Not Predicated Off Threads Per Warp

column20: The optional metric smsp__pcsamp_sample_count could not be found. Collecting it as an additional metric could enable the rule to provide more guidance.

On average, each warp of this workload spends 2.6 cycles being stalled waiting on a fixed latency execution dependency. Typically, this stall reason should be very low and only shows up as a top contributor in already highly optimized kernels. Try to hide the corresponding instruction latencies by increasing the number of active warps, restructuring the code or unrolling loops. Furthermore, consider switching to lower-latency instructions, e.g. by making use of fast math compiler options. This stall type represents about 41.4% of the total average of 6.3 cycles between issuing two instructions.

Check the Warp Stall Sampling (All Samples) table for the top stall locations in your source based on sampling data. The Profiling Guide (https://docs.nvidia.com/nsight-compute/ProfilingGuide/index.html#metrics-reference) provides more details on each stall reason.
